title: Phenomenon 200
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: '''Phenomenon 200''': ''"Sunny Nights"''}}__NOTOC__ {{CSS|User:Pexy0/monovoided.css}} {{CSS|User:Papashfh/Terminal.css}} {{VoidedTheme}} {{Background|file=Sunny Night.jpg}} {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=1|effectiveness=5|danger=5}} ==
Description
== {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Sunny Night.jpg|caption=One of the only images of '''Phemomenon 200'''. Photographer found dead.|hovertext=Spoopy}} '''Phenomenon 200''', also often referred to as '''Sunny Nights'''," "'''Midnight Sun'''," or "'''Lightmoons'''," is an extremely rare and perilous phenomenon encountered on levels where one can minimally ponder the sky which also contain nights. '''Sunny Nights''' appears as an odd, pure white or red sun in the night sky. '''Sunny Nights''' cause various events; among them are entities leaving their hiding spots, third-degree burns, and many others. The rays launched by the sun are concentrated and exceedingly light and hot; therefore, staring at the '''Midnight Sun''' for a small amount of time may permanently blind any wanderer or entity. This phenomenon causes several effects when it occurs. The '''Midnight Sun''' causes several temperature and general anomalies on levels. The electricity in the entirety of the level will malfunction and possibly be cut. The presence of the '''Midnight Sun''' appears to cause all wanderers on the level to hear sounds produced by campfires. Most items composed of leather, wood, or wool are very likely to burn. When the '''Midnight Sun''' appears, the entire level may be blasted by light, creating permanent shadows like those on Hiroshima and Nagasaki. The sheer, sudden appearance of the '''Midnight Sun''' causes most of the elements within the level to be thrown extremely far away. The presence of the '''Midnight Sun''' causes most celestial bodies present within the level to become completely unseeable to the naked eye, and one will require a telescope to observe any celestial bodies other than the '''Midnight Sun'''. Several entities may leave their hiding spots, as aforementioned, due to believing the day has arrived. After the '''Sunny''' '''Night''' ends, the '''Midnight Sun''' will not simply dissipate, fade, or no-clip out of the level; the '''Midnight Sun''' will explode into a massive Super Nova, causing a myriad of objects on the level to burn, be obliterated, or turn into ashes, along with potentially launching most elements within the level extremely far away at a speed of 730 mph (1174,82 kph.). The '''Midnight Sun''' may also rarely become a black hole, which may absorb, spaghettify, and obliterate most of its surroundings, or generally a large section or entire region of one level. The Super Nova explosion of the '''Midnight Sun''' will likely cause a level-wise wildfire that lasts several months, murdering most plant life and fauna at the level. The Super Nova is more likely to cause a nuclear winter, which is exceedingly radioactive, being more radioactive than all nuclear winters recorded, and deadly for a decade, making the level strictly uninhabitable. ==
Physics
== {{Blurimage|align=right|file=This is convincing enough.png|caption=A supposed depiction of the gravitational of the '''Midnight Sun''', however, this is yet to be confirmed, and, likely false.|hovertext=This is better than the last one}} The '''Midnight Sun''' contains a strong gravitational force that attracts nearby celestial bodies and objects to it. Due to this, there have been a myriad of reports of cases of more than eight celestial bodies orbiting a '''Midnight Sun''' before exploding and ultimately destroying the celestial bodies. A '''Midnight Sun''' contains a gravitational force and mass similar to that of a neutron star, although the gravitational force of the '''Midnight Sun''' is slightly stronger. Two '''Midnight Suns''' are rarely seen at the same time at the same level, and more rarely, two '''Midnight Suns''' begin orbiting each other and creating a binary system. This can potentially cause the level to become permanently uninhabitable, therefore murdering most signs of life. The gravitational and magnetic fields of the '''Midnight Sun''' are very strong, and most objects that come near it are launched out. Currently, scientists are attempting to send satellites and telescopes into the '''Midnight Sun''', as their properties are relatively unknown. ==
Variations
== Currently, there are three known variations to a '''Sunny Night''', more specifically, the '''Midnight Sun'''. The variations are as follows: ===Multiplying Suns=== {{Blurimage|align=right|hovertext=Multiaahhhh|file=Lake on Suns.jpg|caption=An image of Multiplying Suns.}} Multiplying Suns are a supposed optic illusion where one may see multiple suns during any time of the day, but mainly at night. Multiplying Suns seem to separate the light emanated from a '''Midnight Sun''' equally among each other while liberating enough to damage one's eyes. Multiplying Suns appear to have a six-pointed star shape. Multiplying Suns have all the properties a '''Midnight Sun''' has, except they are all equally separated from each other while remaining damaging. Once a '''Sunny Night''' ends, the Multiplying Suns will inch towards each other, collide, and enter impact, creating a '''Midnight Sun''' that explodes after several weeks. ===False Suns === False Suns are the rarest among the variations. False Suns appear as darkened suns, almost as black holes. False Suns have a much stronger gravity than the '''Midnight Sun''' and contain a high amount of mass, causing them to be more likely to become black holes. This also causes the level to be potentially attracted to and destroyed by the False Sun. False Suns are cold and cause the entire level to freeze, potentially murdering all its life forms. False Suns have a longer life span compared to the average '''Midnight Sun'''. Currently, scientists are attempting to study False Suns to turn them into a potential weapon and learn how to survive them. ===Partial Suns=== Partial Suns appear as a '''Midnight Sun''' separated between two uneven halves, with one half missing. Partial Suns behave similarly to an average '''Midnight Sun'''; however, the entirety of their properties and stats, such as life span, temperature, mass, and gravity, are halved. Partial Suns generally do not cause much damage and are not very dangerous. Partial Suns are so small that they do not become black holes or explode into supernovas; instead, they become white dwarfs that permanently remain in the sky. Partial Suns do not contain a high quantity of unique properties compared to other variations. ==
Discovery
== '''Sunny Nights''' were discovered on March 9th, 2009, at [[Level 707]], during three simultaneous explorations by the [[M.E.G.]], [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]], and [[Backroom Colonists]]. The three groups were attempting to establish colonies or outposts and searching for resources; they were also attempting to research the strange properties of the level. According to the explorers, while at night they were searching for a temporary base to sleep at, several hours after entering one of the buildings, the phenomenon occurred. The explorers described the happening as an initial discomfort on the part of the explorers, followed by an odd increase in temperatures and items being supposedly launched by wind. Eventually, one of the explorers decided to check what was happening outside, quickly noticing the '''Midnight Sun'''. He warned the others and, against the groups' orders, escaped the level. Fortunately, the '''Midnight Sun''' was too small to explode or turn into a black hole.
